Description: Design fantasy props, structures, symbols, and game assets for immersive stories and shared adventures. Learn digital modeling and creative design.

Travel through American history one doll at a time! Each semester we'll journey alongside two beloved historical girls as the teacher brings their stories to life through engaging read-alouds. Students will create authentic doll clothing and accessories, complete hands-on historical projects, and collect keepsake passport pages as they discover what life was really like long ago. Supply fee includes an 18" doll.

Strengthen your brain while having a blast! Through movement, puzzles, games, coordination challenges, and hands-on activities, students will develop memory, focus, problem-solving, balance, and thinking skills. Each class combines fun brain-training exercises with whole-body movement to strengthen the connections between the brain and body while building confidence and a love of learning.

Challenge your brain in exciting new ways! Students will improve memory, focus, problem-solving, coordination, and critical thinking through brain games, strategy challenges, movement activities, and teamwork. By combining mental exercises with whole-body movement, students strengthen both mind and body while learning skills that support success in every subject.

History comes alive one project at a time! Each week students explore an important event, invention, or historical figure from American history while building a hands-on project connected to the lesson. Through creativity, engineering, storytelling, and discussion, students gain a deeper appreciation for the people and ideas that helped shape our nation.

From stovetop to toaster oven, this class will teach students the essential skills they need to feel confident in the kitchen, trying simple, tasty recipes while learning kitchen safety, measuring, and prepping.

Young chefs will build confidence in the kitchen while learning practical cooking skills they can use for life. Each class includes preparing a simple recipe, practicing kitchen safety, measuring ingredients, following directions, and cleaning up together. Students explore the outdoors through art by creating nature journals, cyanotypes, leaf printing, found-object sculptures, environmental art, and nature-inspired mixed media while developing observation skills and creativity.

Full-day learning community where students grow academically, socially, and personally through hands-on, project-based learning. Each day includes engaging lessons in math, language arts, history, science, and life skills, balanced with creativity, movement, and collaborative iCreation projects. Using our Discover • Create • Become philosophy, students build strong academic foundations while developing character, critical thinking, leadership, and a lifelong love of learning in a supportive mixed-age classroom environment.

Every child learns differently, and this class is designed to celebrate those differences. With a low student-to-teacher ratio, individualized support, and flexible, hands-on activities, students receive the guidance they need to learn, grow, and succeed. Through games, sensory experiences, movement, art, music, life skills, and engaging learning activities, children build confidence, communication, social skills, independence, and a love of learning in a nurturing and encouraging environment.
Students explore visual communication through lettering, illustration, collage, photography, and graphic design while discovering how typography can communicate ideas, personality, and emotion.

Expressions is where communication meets critical thinking. Fashion is more than clothing—it's history, creativity, culture, and self-expression. Students will explore the evolution of fashion through the ages while learning the fundamentals of fashion design, fabrics, sewing, styling, and garment construction. They'll sketch original designs, practice mending and alterations, explore sustainable fashion and upcycling, and transform thrifted finds into unique creations for an end-of-semester fashion showcase. Whether you're interested in design, sewing, or discovering your own style, this hands-on class brings fashion to life.
Students build confidence through drawing and painting while learning composition, color theory, perspective, observation, and creative expression using a variety of traditional media.

Get ready to dive into the world of educational games where strategy, skill, and smarts come together! In this hands-on, in-person class, students explore a new academic subject each week through board games, card games, and group challenges that boost logic, communication, and teamwork.

Learn to play one of the world's most versatile instruments! Students will develop fundamental guitar skills such as chords, rhythm, strumming, music reading, and simple melodies while building confidence through fun songs and group playing. Whether you're brand new or have some experience, this class provides an encouraging environment to grow as a musician.

Celebrate America's people, history, symbols, and ideals through stories, songs, games, crafts, and hands-on activities. Learn about various artists and illustrators and various art styles such as children's books, vintage, cute characters, fantasy & realistic. We'll create a variety of projects that include both traditional & digital art methods to create a portfolio of characters, environment and page layouts.

Pack your bags—we're taking a road trip across America! Students will "visit" every state while exploring its geography, history, culture, famous landmarks, music, food, and traditions. Through games, crafts, cooking, maps, and hands-on activities, they'll discover what makes each state unique while gaining a deeper appreciation for our country.

Build a strong musical foundation while learning to play the keyboard. Students will develop beginning piano skills, music reading, rhythm, technique, chords, and simple songs in a fun and encouraging group environment. Keyboard is an excellent starting point for understanding music and developing lifelong musical skills. This class does not include an instrument, but students are welcome to bring their own or arrange a rental.

Bring your LEGO creations to life! Students will design, build, and program LEGO robots while learning the fundamentals of coding, engineering, and robotics. Through fun challenges and friendly in-class competitions, they'll strengthen problem-solving, teamwork, and creative thinking—without the commitment of an official competition team.

Discover the amazing world of engineering through LEGO®! Each week students tackle exciting building challenges that strengthen creativity, teamwork, problem-solving, and critical thinking. From simple machines and structures to imaginative inventions, every class encourages kids to design, build, test, improve, and create.

FIRST LEGO League Explore Competition Team. Calling all young engineers! In this year's BioGlow season, students will work together to explore the amazing ways living things use light while designing creative LEGO models and simple programming projects inspired by nature. Through guided STEM challenges, teamwork, storytelling, and hands-on building, students will develop problem-solving, creativity, and engineering skills while preparing for an exciting end-of-season FIRST LEGO League Explore showcase. FIRST LEGO League Challenge Competition Team. Take your robotics skills to the next level as part of our competitive FIRST LEGO League Challenge team! This season's BioGlow theme invites students to explore the fascinating ways living things use light and to develop innovative solutions inspired by the natural world. Team members will design, build, and program LEGO robots, complete official challenge missions, research a real-world problem, and prepare presentations for competition. Along the way, students will develop engineering, coding, teamwork, communication, and problem-solving skills in an exciting, hands-on environment where innovation comes to life. Extra Meetings: Additional practices may be scheduled outside of class as competitions approach. Competition Fees: Team members will share the cost of competition registration and related expenses. Fees will vary depending on the number of team members, competitions attended, and travel requirements. At minimum, the team will participate in one official competition. Fundraising opportunities may be available to help offset costs. Supply fee includes a team t-shirt plus first competition registration fee.

Step into America's fight for freedom through exciting stories, hands-on activities, and historical adventures. Students will explore the people, events, and principles of the American Revolution while discovering the courage, sacrifice, and ideas that shaped our nation. History comes alive through projects, discussions, games, and interactive experiences.

Become an inventor as you explore how machines and robots work! Students will design, build, and experiment with gears, levers, pulleys, motors, simple robotics, and other mechanical systems through exciting hands-on projects. Along the way, they'll develop engineering, creativity, teamwork, and problem-solving skills while discovering how everyday machines make our world work.

Build, experiment, and discover the science behind amazing machines! Students will create a variety of hands-on mechanical projects while exploring concepts like motion, force, energy, gears, levers, pulleys, and simple engineering. Every class encourages curiosity, creativity, and the inventor's mindset as students learn by making.

One person's trash is another inventor's treasure! Students will transform recycled and everyday materials into creative inventions, useful projects, works of art, and engineering challenges. Along the way, they'll develop creativity, design thinking, problem-solving, and an appreciation for reusing materials in imaginative ways.
Learn how messages shape people. Analyze media, question persuasion, identify bias, and think critically in a world driven by algorithms and digital influence.

Discover the tiny world of miniatures! Students will create adorable small-scale projects using a variety of materials and techniques while developing creativity, fine motor skills, and attention to detail. From tiny rooms and foods to miniature gardens and imaginative creations, each class offers a fun new opportunity to build something little with lots of personality.

Young chefs will build confidence and independence while preparing simple recipes using child-friendly, hands-on techniques. Get ready for an adventure in Earth and Life Sciences! In Nature Quest, students will explore the wonders of the natural world through hands-on experiments, outdoor investigations, and exciting activities, all aligned with Utah's 4th-8th grade science standards.

Adventure awaits! Students will learn practical outdoor skills such as navigation, shelter building, fire safety, knot tying, first aid, nature identification, tracking, outdoor cooking, and wilderness survival. Through hands-on challenges and team activities, they'll build confidence, resilience, leadership, and a greater appreciation for the outdoors.

Create what inspires you! Students are free to work on their own artistic projects while receiving guidance, encouragement, and instruction from the teacher as needed. Learn how economies function, how incentives and global events shape trade, markets, policy, and everyday life.

Discover science through all five senses! Students will investigate the world around them with hands-on experiments, sensory activities, creative projects, and playful exploration. The ukulele is the perfect instrument for beginning musicians! Students will learn chords, strumming patterns, rhythm, and simple songs while developing musical confidence and enjoying the fun of making music together. This cheerful class makes learning an instrument approachable and rewarding. This class does not include an instrument, but students are welcome to bring their own or arrange a rental.

Find your voice and build confidence through singing! Students will develop healthy vocal technique, breathing, pitch, harmony, rhythm, and performance skills while singing a variety of fun songs. Whether singing solo or with a group, students will grow as musicians while learning to express themselves through music.

Pack your (imaginary) suitcase, we are off to explore the world! In World Wanderer, students will learn basic geography skills like using maps and understanding continents while visiting countries across the globe. Through stories, crafts, music, traditions, and sometimes even food, they will discover the beauty of different cultures. Students will keep maps and worksheets in their personal binders and complete a country project to share their favorite discoveries with the class.

Become part of the iCreate media team! Students will plan, write, edit, design, and publish the monthly iCreate newspaper while also producing a weekly video news broadcast for our community. Along the way, they'll develop skills in journalism, interviewing, photography, video production, graphic design, teamwork, and communication while reporting on the exciting things happening throughout iCreate.

The Summit Program is a mentored, project-based high school experience for students who are ready to take ownership of their education. Designed for self-motivated teens, the program combines individualized learning plans, real-world projects, mentorship, internships, and leadership opportunities to help students develop the knowledge, skills, and confidence they'll need beyond high school. Rather than simply earning credits, students build a portfolio of meaningful work while preparing for college, careers, entrepreneurship, and life. Must apply and be approved for this program.
